2605.12299.title: GKnow: Measuring the Entanglement of Gender Bias and Factual Gender
2605.12299.paper_summary: Recent works have analyzed the impact of individual components of neural networks on gendered predictions, often with a focus on mitigating gender bias. However, mechanistic interpretations of gender tend to (i) focus on a very specific gender-related task, such as gendered pronoun prediction, or (ii) fail to distinguish between the production of factually gendered outputs (the correct assumption of gender given a word that carries gender as a semantic property) and gender biased outputs (based on a stereotype). To address these issues, we curate \gknow, a benchmark to assess gender knowledge and gender bias in language models across different types of gender-related predictions. \gknow allows us to identify and analyze circuits and individual neurons responsible for gendered predictions. We test the impact of neuron ablation on benchmarks for disentangling stereotypical and factual gender (DiFair and the test set of GKnow), as well as StereoSet. Results show that gender bias and factual gender are severely entangled on the level of both circuits and neurons, entailing that ablation is an unreliable debiasing method. Furthermore, we show that benchmarks for evaluating gender bias can hide the decrease in factual gender knowledge that accompanies neuron ablation. We curate GKnow as a contribution to the continuous development of robust gender bias benchmarks.
2605.12299.paper_summary_zh: 最近的研究分析了神經網絡中各個組件對性別預測的影響，通常專注於減少性別偏見。然而，性別的機械解釋往往 (i) 專注於非常特定的性別相關任務，例如性別代名詞預測，或 (ii) 無法區分事實性別輸出（根據帶有性別作為語義屬性的單詞正確假設的性別）和性別偏見輸出（基於刻板印象）。為了解決這些問題，我們策劃了 \gknow，一個基準，用於評估語言模型中性別知識和性別偏見在不同類型的性別相關預測中的表現。 \gknow 使我們能夠識別和分析負責性別預測的電路和個別神經元。我們測試了神經元切除對於區分刻板印象性別和事實性別的基準（DiFair 和 GKnow 測試集）的影響，以及 StereoSet。結果顯示，性別偏見和事實性別在電路和神經元層面上嚴重交織，這意味著切除是一種不可靠的去偏見方法。此外，我們顯示評估性別偏見的基準可能隱藏了伴隨神經元切除而減少的事實性別知識。我們策劃了 GKnow，作為對穩健性別偏見基準持續發展的貢獻。
2605.12299.author: Leonor Veloso et.al.
